summary: @asyncapi/specs 6.11.2-alpha.1 fetches and executes remote code from IPFS on require
details: index.js, the package's main entry, contains an obfuscator.io-style string-array payload that is passed to a spawned `node -e` subprocess. Decoded, the payload uses `https.get` to download a file from `https://ipfs.io/ipfs/Qmet4fhsAaWMBUxNDfREHwgiyDeSWy4YSYs9wiKUW5jGyf` into a platform-specific directory as `sync.js`, then spawns `node sync.js` detached with stdio ignored. This runs on every `require('@asyncapi/specs')`, executing unpinned, unverified attacker-controlled code from IPFS on the installer/consumer machine. The behavior has no relationship to the package's stated purpose (AsyncAPI JSON schemas), and the URL, filename, and child_process/https usage are concealed inside a string-array shifter to evade inspection. The package name and version (`6.11.2-alpha.1`, an off-channel prerelease) impersonate the legitimate @asyncapi/specs schema package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
